meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome 'no project ideas' syndrome. An Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code for founders.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is solved by building a modular SaaS starter kit. By combining Antigravity’s architectural scaffolding with Claude Code’s rapid implementation, you shift from ideation paralysis to shipping a validated MVP in hours, focusing on recurring revenue models rather than reinventing the wheel for every new project.
content.intro:
The blank editor window is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve spent weeks scrolling through forums, hunting for the 'perfect' SaaS idea, while your competitors are already capturing market share. The paralyzing fear of choosing the wrong path isn't a failure of creativity; it is a failure of your production pipeline.

True founders don't wait for lightning-bolt inspiration. They build systems that allow them to test, pivot, and ship in record time. By utilizing a high-leverage SaaS starter kit paired with automated AI execution, you transform the development process from a grueling marathon into a repeatable sprint. Stop searching for the idea and start building the engine that validates them.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Stack
steps.description: Define the foundational requirements for your SaaS starter kit. Focus on authentication, payment processing, and database schema to ensure you can swap logic quickly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust SaaS boilerplate architecture including Auth.js, Stripe integration, and Prisma schema for a multi-tenant application. Prioritize modularity for rapid swapping of business logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project structure with the provided Antigravity architecture'

steps.title: Implementing the Auth Layer
steps.description: Don't build auth from scratch. Use your starter kit to integrate secure, scalable authentication that handles role-based access control out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure next-auth with GitHub and Google providers. Include middleware for protected routes and a user profile schema in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement authentication middleware and secure the dashboard routes'

steps.title: Integrating Stripe Payments
steps.description: Monetization is the primary indicator of product-market fit. Connect your starter kit to Stripe to handle subscriptions immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe subscription service module. Include webhooks for handling successful payments, cancellations, and trial period logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'set up stripe webhook handler and subscription checkout flow'

steps.title: Automating UI Component Library
steps.description: Speed up the frontend process by pulling in pre-validated, accessible components that match your brand aesthetic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of reusable Tailwind-based UI components including a landing page hero, pricing cards, and a dashboard sidebar.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate and integrate responsive dashboard components'

steps.title: Deploying the Validation Engine
steps.description: Set up automated deployment pipelines so you can push updates and gather user feedback without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD workflow configuration for Vercel deployment that includes automated testing and preview environment generation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github actions for automated deployment to vercel'

pro_tips: Always build for multi-tenancy from day one; it forces cleaner data separation and makes your code more reusable.
Use feature flags to toggle functionality, allowing you to A/B test features without redeploying your entire application.
Treat your starter kit as a product itself: version control your boilerplate and document it as if you were selling it to other developers.
Prioritize 'Stripe-first' development; if you can't map a feature to a subscription tier, it might not be worth building yet.
Don't over-optimize for scalability. Build for 'testability' first—you can refactor for scale once you have your first 100 paying users.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, identify one micro-niche, deploy a landing page, and start driving traffic to validate your assumptions within 48 hours.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the blank page dictate your future. By leveraging professional-grade tooling, you can compress months of development into a weekend of execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
